business.headline: Centre pushes GST Appellate Tribunal appeal filing deadline by 1 monthThe new deadline is July 31, 2026, replacing the earlier deadline of June 30, 2026, which had been notified in September last year.

Indians love gold. So why is there a rush to sell it?Gold still occupies a special place in Indian households, but rising prices and changing investor behaviour are transforming it from a family heirloom into a modern financial asset.

8th Pay Commission: Salary and allowance data to be submitted ahead of pay revisionThe information is expected to help the Commission prepare recommendations on revised pay scales, allowances, overall government expenditure and long-term financial sustainability.

Filed your ITR? Complete this step within 30 days of filing or it may be rejectedClicked 'Submit' on your ITR? Don't relax just yet. You still need to complete one crucial step to make your tax return valid. Have a look.

Guaranteed returns, zero risk: How $60 million investment scam fooled 300 peoplePaul Regan pleaded guilty after prosecutors accused him of running a $60 million Ponzi scheme that drew more than 300 US investors with promises of guaranteed returns and zero risk. The case shows how emotional reassurance, false protection claims and trust can override basic questions about investment risk.

Sensex, Nifty erase early gains as IT stocks drag; Q1 earnings in focusBenchmark indices erased opening gains on Tuesday as IT stocks pulled the market lower. Attention is now shifting to June-quarter earnings, with analysts watching financials, healthcare and power.

Gold prices see biggest monthly drop in 18 years. Good time to buy?Gold prices have slid sharply from record highs as investors weigh higher US rates and a stronger dollar. The correction has reopened the buying debate, with upcoming US data likely to shape the next move.

Microsoft loses $570 billion in a month. Is Wall Street losing faith in AI boom?Microsoft has lost more than $570 billion in market value this month, marking its worst monthly performance since the dot-com era. Investors are increasingly questioning whether the software giant's massive AI investments will generate enough profits to justify the spending.

Japan's yen falls to 40-year low against US dollarThe yen fell to its weakest level against the US dollar since 1986, reviving expectations of fresh market intervention by Tokyo. The slide reflects the widening US-Japan rate gap and keeps pressure on Japan's economy and global investors.

Old vs new tax regime: How standard deduction works and who benefitsAs the ITR filing season gathers pace, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series breaks down key tax benefits. In the first part, we explain the standard deduction and how it works under the old and new tax regimes.

HDFC Bank appoints ex-poll body chief Rajiv Kumar as chairman; RBI nod awaitedHDFC Bank has appointed former Finance Secretary and ex-Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar as its new chairman, subject to RBI approval, replacing Atanu Chakraborty.
Yen sinks to 40-year low, dollar slips but stays near 13-month highThe Japanese yen weakened to its lowest levels against the US currency since 1986.

Govt to lift petrol and diesel sales curbs from July 1 as supply concerns easeThe precautionary measures were introduced to protect domestic fuel supplies at a time when uncertainty in the global energy market was increasing.

From ITR deadlines to passport fees: 5 financial changes from July 1 you must knowWhether you're planning to file your ITR, apply for a passport or simply keep track of your finances, July 1 brings a few important changes you shouldn't ignore. Here are five key financial updates that could matter to you. Which ones will affect you the most?

Markets snap winning streak as weak monsoon worries trigger profit bookingThe BSE Sensex fell 372.10 points, or 0.48%, to close at 76,728.37, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 109.75 points, or 0.46%, to settle at 23,946.25.

Amazon, Flipkart quick commerce push dents Blinkit and SwiggyAmazon and Flipkart are rapidly expanding their quick commerce networks across India. The push has deepened investor concerns over profitability, valuations and a prolonged price war.

EPFO 3.0: 5 PF mistakes that could delay your transfer when switching jobsChanging jobs comes with plenty of paperwork, and transferring your PF is one task you shouldn't ignore. While EPFO 3.0 promises a faster and smoother process, a few simple mistakes could still delay your money. Are you making any of them?

Gold price has crashed nearly Rs 50,000 from its peak. Will prices fall further?Gold has slipped sharply from its MCX record high as investors reassess the US rate outlook. The fall has given buyers some relief, but volatility remains tied to inflation, oil and Federal Reserve signals.

BAT to slash 9,000 jobs amid shift to smoke-free productsThe move is part of the company's wider plan to reduce costs while investing more in smoke-free products such as vaping devices and nicotine pouches.

Nearly Rs 9,000 crore in unclaimed insurance: How to check if any is yoursNearly Rs 8,974 crore in insurance money remains unclaimed with insurers across India. Old policies, missing nominee details and outdated records could leave rightful dues untouched for years.

entertainment.headline: Parasakthi director Sudha Kongara moves court against makers over unpaid dues Parasakthi director Sudha Kongara has moved the Madras High Court over alleged unpaid remuneration of Rs 8.39 crore for the Sivakarthikeyan-starrer. The dispute has prompted the court to indicate that the film's satellite release should be put on hold until July 8.

Kayadu Lohar is South cinema's busiest new star: 7 films, including Suriya, Nani After making a mark with Dragon, Kayadu Lohar has built an impressive multilingual slate. Here's a look at the seven films the actor has lined up next.

Salman Khan's sea-facing home gets redevelopment nod but will he move out of Galaxy? Salman Khan's family has received redevelopment approval for their sea-facing Bandra property from the Maharashtra Coastal Zone Management Authority. The property has been with them for over a decade.

Dia Mirza says son schooled coconut vendor on plastic use, sparks 'privilege' debate Dia Mirza recounted how her son Avyaan asked a coconut delivery worker to remove plastic packaging and take it back. The clip went viral and drew criticism online, with users calling the exchange entitled and insensitive.

Actor Raghava Lawrence opts out of Trichy East bid, keeps political future open Actor-director Raghava Lawrence said he will not contest from Trichy East amid speculation over a TVK candidacy. He said political dharma requires grassroots workers to be recognised first, while leaving open a future entry elsewhere.

Ahmed Khan shares real reason behind Sanjay Dutt exiting Welcome To The Jungle Director Ahmed Khan opened up on the reason behind actor Sanjay Dutt's exit from Welcome To The Jungle. He said Dutt stayed supportive and helped reorganise the casting of the film.

Wasn't eligible for home loan: Shreyas Talpade reveals how SRK changed his life Actor Shreyas Talpade recalled how his work on the film Om Shanti Om helped him secure a home loan in Mumbai. Director Farah Khan said she had not realised the film had played such a significant role in his life.

Bhai Tera Star Hai: Actor Raghav Juyal's next is about Bollywood and stardom Raghav Juyal has unveiled his first look as Ajay Singh from Bhai Tera Star Hai. Sharing the poster, the actor also announced that the teaser will be out soon.

Voicemails for Isabelle: Popular Netflix rom-com is really a love letter to sisters Voicemails for Isabelle may be packaged as a Netflix romance, but its biggest love story belongs to sisters Jill and Isabelle. Here's why the film's emotional core has little to do with romance.

Alia Bhatt recalls 5-hour-long Latent 2 shoot, says was stressed from laughing Alia Bhatt said she had fun filming India's Got Latent Season 2 with Sharvari. The actor-duo appeared on the premiere episode of the season to promote their upcoming film Alpha.

Ranveer Singh finally begins prep for zombie thriller Pralay, to shoot in September Ranveer Singh is reportedly preparing to begin shooting for Jai Mehta's zombie thriller Pralay in 2026. As per reports, a major Australia schedule is planned.

Yash answers Toxic's 'Where are the ladies?' buzz, teases July 1 surprise After months of speculation, Toxic has finally teased the arrival of its leading ladies. Yash responded to the makers' "Where are the ladies?" post with a cryptic update.

Bas, guys: Sonakshi Sinha loses patience with paps outside Mumbai restaurant. Video Actor Sonakshi Sinha firmly asked paparazzi to stop filming her after a family dinner outing with husband Zaheer Iqbal and father Shatrughan Sinha. A video of the interaction has since gone viral on social media.

Kannada actor Divya Suresh alleges street harassment in Bengaluru, questions safety Kannada actor Divya Suresh said an unidentified man harassed her and her cousin while they walked to their car in Bengaluru late on Sunday night. She shared the episode on social media and questioned the safety assurances made to women in the city.

He wants kids badly: Akanksha Chamola on what broke her marriage with Gaurav Khanna On Lock Upp Sach Ya Sazaa, actor Akanksha Chamola said her divorce from Gaurav Khanna stemmed from their differing views on having children. She said they remain on talking terms but are parting because they want different futures.

Psychological horror Backrooms returns to theatres with 15 mins of new footage A24 will re-release Backrooms in cinemas on 3 July with 15 minutes of bonus footage. The new cut arrives after the low-budget horror film became the studio's biggest box-office hit.

Jr NTR's new film sparks Lord Murugan row: Why 'Born in the North' angered Tamils A first-look poster from Jr NTR and Trivikram Srinivas's upcoming film has set off an online argument over Lord Murugan's origins. The row has widened into a debate on Tamil cultural identity, mythology and how the deity is represented on screen.

Zeenat Aman, 74, backs live-in relationship: Better 5 happy years than 15 miserable Veteran actor Zeenat Aman has reaffirmed her support for live-in relationships, saying couples should assess compatibility before marriage. She has continued to defend her views, by emphasising commitment, adjustment and compromise.

Dhurandhar's action director joins Salman Khan's SVC 63? Here's what we know Salman Khan's upcoming film SVC 63 has reportedly roped in Korean action director Sea Young Oh, popularly known as Mr Oh, who previously worked on Dhurandhar, Kill and War.

general news.headline: Ethanol mixing an experiment, results by next year: Govt to SC amid E20 worriesIn a clarification to India Today, Attorney General R Venkataramani said that the "20% mix of ethanol is a policy decision that is not likely to change. How much ethanol is made available to companies may go up or down depending on demand and other factors."

Siya Goyal's 'lawyer' sues her brother for Rs 10 crore over defamationAdvocate Aashutosh Srivastava has sent a Rs 10 crore defamation notice to Sahil Goyal, accusing the brother of murder accused Siya Goyal of making false statements. Srivastava's notice was in response to Sahil Goyal saying that the lawyer had used deception to get Siya to sign legal documents.

Gor says Indian Minister bullish on US ties; Ask Jaishankar, Democrat scoffsCalifornia Democrat Ro Khanna blasted US President Donald Trump for destroying ties with India, calling it the lowest in 30 years, while speaking at an event. He said Trump's policies have eroded India's trust.

Neville Singham, probed in India for China propaganda, faces investigation in US nowThe US Department of Justice has launched a federal grand jury investigation into tech millionaire Neville Roy Singham over allegations that he helped channel millions of dollars illegally to left-wing media outlets and activist organisations in the US. Singham is being probed in India under anti-terror law for trying to spread Chinese propaganda.

'Kalema' flags used by IS, al-Qaida appear in Bangladesh amid FIFA World Cup frenzyBlack-and-white 'Kalema' flags have appeared across Bangladesh during the FIFA World Cup season, raising an alarm in security circles. Analysts said it points to the rising footprint of foreign extremist groups in Bangladesh.

Only AC government buses in Tamil Nadu from now, CM Vijay says: Transport MinisterTransport Minister Vijay Tamilan Parthiban said Tamil Nadu will henceforth procure only AC buses since Chief Minister Vijay wants people to travel more widely and comfortably.

Delhi man invites woman out for momos, then demands sexual favours; case lodgedIn her complaint, the woman alleged that the accused, who was known to her, lured her into stepping out by saying they would go out to eat momos. She said he later insisted on stopping to refill the car with CNG before driving towards a deserted stretch.

If they wake up in a bad mood…: US envoy throws shade at India's neighboursUS Ambassador Sergio Gor said India is located in a tough spot in the world, while emphasising that the ties between Washington and New Delhi will be sustainable because both sides have many commonalities.

Satellite captures 1,500-km-long monsoon rain band from Bengal to KashmirSatellite imagery has captured a monsoon trough stretching from the northern Bay of Bengal to Jammu and Kashmir. Its expected southward shift could boost thunderstorms and help the monsoon advance across north India.

Shashank Singh denies assault allegations: Cook was taking private videosPunjab Kings cricketer Shashank Singh has denied allegations of assault and wrongful confinement made by his former cook. A day after an FIR was registered against him and his father in Bhopal, Shashank responded to the allegations publicly.

Muharram poisoning case: Broken home, social isolation led accused to plot revengePolice say the accused distributed alleged poison-filled capsules during a Muharram procession in Mumbai. Investigators are probing whether personal grievances, isolation and mental health issues shaped the alleged plot.

Indians love gold. So why is there a rush to sell it?Gold still occupies a special place in Indian households, but rising prices and changing investor behaviour are transforming it from a family heirloom into a modern financial asset.

Cheating can happen by mistake: Actor Ram Kapoor defends infidelity on Lock UppRam Kapoor and Akanksha Chamola clashed on Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa over whether cheating can ever be a mistake in marriage. The exchange put trust, forgiveness and accountability at the centre of the show's latest talking point.

He's like me, doesn't sleep: US envoy on when Trump said to call PM Modi at 6 amUS Ambassador Sergio Gor said Donald Trump once suggested calling Prime Minister Narendra Modi at 6 am in India during an event. He said the episode reflected a personal rapport that sits apart from formal diplomatic scheduling.

Kerala man who posed as anti-drug activist arrested after MDMA found at his homeA man who projected himself as an anti-drug campaigner while being involved in drug use and trafficking was arrested after police recovered 1.590 grams of MDMA from his possession.

Usernames coming to WhatsApp, CEO Kunal Shah says reserve your name nowWhatsApp will soon let users connect without revealing their phone numbers. CEO Kunal Shah has announced that usernames are coming to the messaging platform and is encouraging users to reserve their preferred names before the feature rolls out more widely later this year.

Not just 'South ki Imli', kokum is now flavouring ramen, cocktails and coffeeChefs and bartenders are taking kokum from coastal curries into ramen, chocolate, coffee and cocktails. The shift reflects a wider push to rediscover regional Indian ingredients beyond their traditional homes.

Why is Punjab's anti-sacrilege law facing Akal Takht heat? 5 big points hereThe Akal Takht, Sikhism's highest temporal seat of power, has asked the Punjab government to put its new anti-sacrilege law on hold and revise the disputed clauses within a month. Why was the new law brought in, and why has it faced criticism from the Akal Takht?

US Supreme Court vastly expands Trump's presidential powers in landmark rulingThe US Supreme Court ruling significantly expands presidential authority over independent federal agencies and overturns a landmark 1935 precedent that had protected many regulators from being removed at will.

UP man Ayush Malik, who converted to Islam for lover, returns to HinduismAyush Malik, the Shamli man whose conversion to Islam led to an investigation under Uttar Pradesh's anti-conversion law, has returned to Hinduism, saying he did so voluntarily after considering his family's distress.

A 64-year-old intern went viral. Is India retiring its smartest talent too soon?A viral video of a 64-year-old intern has reignited debate over retirement in India. As people live longer and healthier lives, should retirement become a choice instead of a fixed age? Experts say the country's untapped silver workforce could become one of its biggest economic and social strengths.

Not our job to run temple: VHP washes hands of Champat Rai in donation theft rowThe Vishwa Hindu Parishad's remarks come as scrutiny intensifies over the functioning of the Ram Mandir trust after it was established that donations made at the Ram temple in Ayodhya were stolen in a systematic manner.

The Kill Bill inspiration behind Naomi Osaka's show-stopping Wimbledon lookNaomi Osaka arrived on Wimbledon court in an all-white, kimono-inspired ensemble before beating Elsa Jacquemot in straight sets. The Hana Yagi-designed look drew attention for blending Japanese heritage, as well as a look from a Hollywood film etched in everyone's memory.

Will cut off those hands: Pak minister's warning to India over Indus watersAddressing a joint press conference with Climate Change Minister Musadik Malik, Information Minister Attaullah Tarar insisted that the Indus Waters Treaty remained legally binding and could not be suspended, revoked or amended unilaterally.

sports.headline: Shashank Singh denies assault allegations: Cook was taking private videos Punjab Kings cricketer Shashank Singh has denied allegations of assault and wrongful confinement made by his former cook. A day after an FIR was registered against him and his father in Bhopal, Shashank responded to the allegations publicly.

Day 2 schedule: Serena Williams returns to Centre Court, centre stage Serena Williams returns to Centre Court against Maya Joint as Wimbledon's Day 2 schedule takes shape. Her comeback headlines a packed slate featuring Iga Swiatek, Alexander Zverev and Elena Rybakina.

Noisy Mexico fans ruin Ecuador's sleep ahead of knockout clash Hundreds of Mexico supporters gathered outside Ecuador's team hotel in Mexico City ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against the co-hosts. The rowdy fans created noise with chants, horns and speakers in an apparent attempt to disrupt the visitors' sleep.

Defiant Koeman blasts reporters after Netherlands crash out in Round of 32 Netherlands coach Ronald Koeman launched a strong defence of his tactical approach despite his side's FIFA World Cup Round of 32 exit against Morocco. Netherlands crashed out of the tournament after failing to convert in the penalty shootouts.

Watch: Djokovic's violin joke goes viral as daughter Tara can't stop smiling Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic's violin celebration returned after his Wimbledon win, prompting a shy reaction from daughter Tara. The light-hearted moment followed a testing four-set victory over Wu Yibing in the opening round.

Penalties haunt Netherlands once again as Dutch crash out in R32 Penalty shootouts haunted Netherlands once again as Ronald Koeman's team were knocked out of the FIFA World Cup 2026. A disastrous spell of spot kicks saw them miss 3 chances, sealing their fate in the tournament.

Explained: Did VAR call send Germany crashing out of FIFA World Cup? FIFA World Cup 2026: Germany believed Jonathan Tah had sent them through to the Round of 16 before VAR intervened dramatically in extra time. The overturned goal sparked fury, scrutiny of goalkeeper protection, and another painful early tournament exit for the four-time champions.

Harmanpreet Kaur to captain India as BCCI announces Asian Games squad Harmanpreet Kaur will continue to lead India in T20Is. The BCCI on Tuesday announced a 15-member squad for women's cricket at Asian Games 2026, scheduled to be held in Aichi-Nagoya, Japan, in September 2026.

FIR against cricketer Shashank Singh and retired IPS father for assaulting cook Punjab Kings player Shashank Singh, his retired IPS father and their driver have been booked after their cook alleged assault and abuse in Bhopal. The complaint includes claims of phone seizure and visible injuries, with police now investigating the case.

Australia eye another final as West Indies plot ambush Australia enter the Women's T20 World Cup semi-final as overwhelming favourites after an unbeaten campaign, but a resilient West Indies side will back its experienced match-winners to spring a knockout upset.

Paraguay celebrates historic World Cup win vs Germany with public holiday Paraguay's stunning World Cup win over Germany has sparked nationwide celebrations, with President Santiago Pena declaring a public holiday to mark La Albirroja's historic Round of 16 qualification.

Gakpo in tears, scoring days after unborn son's death, teammates rally around himFIFA World Cup 2026: Cody Gakpo scored for the Netherlands in their soul-crushing defeat to Morocco in the Round of 32 game, days after his partner announced the loss of their unborn son. The goal sparked an emotional response and drew support from teammates and fans.

Morocco 3-2 Netherlands in penalties: Disastrous Dutch crash out of World CupNetherlands (NED) vs Morocco (MAR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: A disastrous penalty shootout sees Netherlands crash out of the FIFA World Cup. 3 misses from the Dutch saw Morocco reach the Round of 16, where they will face co-hosts Canada. Earlier, one goal each from Cody Gakpo and Isa Diop, saw the game go into Extra Time.

Paraguay's finest hour: Giants Germany sent packing in Round of 32Paraguay held Germany for 120 minutes before winning 4-3 in the shootout after a 1-1 draw. The result handed Germany their first World Cup shootout defeat and gave Paraguay a landmark victory.

Neymar hits back at German economist who predicted Japan would beat BrazilNeymar took a playful swipe at German economist Joachim Klement after Brazil's 2-1 win over Japan proved his World Cup prediction wrong, with the Brazil star's post quickly going viral.

Djokovic sees off Wu Yibing in Round 1 as proposal adds to Wimbledon spectacleNovak Djokovic overcame a spirited Wu Yibing in four sets to reach the Wimbledon second round, with a surprise marriage proposal in the stands and his witty request for a wedding invitation stealing attention.

Why did Neymar not play in the Round of 32 clash?Brazil vs Japan: Carlo Ancelotti revealed Neymar was kept on the bench against Japan as a contingency for extra time, with Brazil's tactical halftime switch inspiring a dramatic 2-1 comeback to reach the Round of 16.

Japan coach, players bow down to fans in apology as Brazil end World Cup dreamFIFA World Cup 2026: Japan coach Hajime Moriyasu and his players bowed before their fans after Brazil's dramatic 2-1 win ended the Samurai Blue's FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign in an emotional Round of 32 farewell.

West Indies have nothing to lose, will be fearless vs Australia: Hayley MatthewsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, AUS vs WI: West Indies skipper Hayley Matthews said that her team will be fearless when they face Sophie Molineux's Australia on Tuesday in the first semifinal at the Oval.

GER vs PAR Highlights: Paraguay does the unthinkable, knock out GermanyGermany vs Paraguay Highlights FIFA World Cup 2026: (GER 1 (3) - 1(4) PAR) Paraguay have stunned the four-time world champions, knocking them out of the FIFA World Cup. After, the game remained level by the end of extra-time, Paraguay toppled the German giants over penalties.

Don Carlo's big-game magic sends Brazil past brave Japan into Round of 16FIFA World Cup 2026: Brazil came from behind to beat a brave and well-organised Japan side 2-1 in Houston, sealing their place in the World Cup Round of 16. Gabriel Martinelli struck a dramatic stoppage-time winner after Casemiro had earlier cancelled out Kaishu Sano's first-half opener.

Australia wary of Hayley Matthews, Deandra Dottin threats in semis vs West IndiesWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, AUS vs WI: Ash Gardner said that Australia need to be careful with West Indies having match-winners like Deandra Dottin and Hayley Matthews going into the semis on Tuesday at the Oval.

Jannik Sinner explains playing with blood on shoe after nasty fall at WimbledonJannik Sinner brushed off concerns after playing through a bleeding toe to stage a remarkable five-set comeback against Miomir Kecmanovic in the Wimbledon 2026.

Saudi football's big ambitions hit wall as FA president Al-Misehal resigns after WC exitFIFA World Cup 2026: Saudi Arabia's World Cup exit has cost Yasser Al-Misehal his job after seven years in charge. The resignation exposes fresh questions over a lavish football project still short on tournament substance.

Jannik Sinner survives huge 5-set scare, battles injury to reach Round 2Jannik Sinner battled through a gruelling 3-hour-30-minute five-set win over Miomir Kecmanovic at Wimbledon 2026, overcoming an injury scare and a tiebreak setback to advance to Round 2.

BRA 2-1 JPN Highlights: Late Martinelli goal sends Brazil through to RO16Brazil (BRA) vs Japan (JPN) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (BRA 2-1 JPN) Brazil came from behind to beat Japan 2-1 in a thrilling Round of 32 clash. Casemiro cancelled out Japan's opener before Gabriel Martinelli struck deep into stoppage time to send the Selecao into the Round of 16.

No Alcaraz, no free pass: Vijay Amritraj sees tough Wimbledon for Jannik SinnerDespite Carlos Alcaraz's injury-enforced absence, Vijay Amritraj believes defending champion Jannik Sinner will face a stern test in his bid to retain the Wimbledon title, insisting there are no free passes at the All England Club.

How Australia flipped the script to send India crashing out: Ash Gardner revealsWomen's T20 World Cup: Ash Gardner revealed Australia overcame the pressure of a steep chase by targeting favourable match-ups, breaking the innings into smaller phases and backing their batting depth to attack India's bowlers.

Maja Chwalinska goes from French Open fairytale to Wimbledon heartbreakMaja Chwalinska's Wimbledon campaign ended in heartbreaking fashion after the Polish star squandered a set, a 5-2 lead and a match point before losing to Mananchaya Sawangkaew in the first round.

India vs Pakistan unlikely at 2028 Olympics. Cricket qualification rules explainedCricket's LA28 qualification rules have placed India firmly on track for Los Angeles while pushing Pakistan towards a treacherous global qualifier. By heavily prioritising regional rankings, the newly unveiled roadmap drastically raises the stakes for every bilateral series—leaving a blockbuster India-Pakistan Olympic clash far from guaranteed.

Day 1 Highlights: Djokovic overcomes Yibing, Mirra Andreeva beats Magda LinetteWimbledon 2026, Day 1 Highlights: Jannik Sinner has beaten Miomir Kecmanovic in five sets on Centre Court and advanced to the second round. Aryna Sabalenka defeated Teodora Kostovic in the first round. Later, Novak Djokovic beat China's Wu Yibing.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
